topic.what: Quantitative confirmation of Currier's 1976 A/B language distinction in Voynich manuscript via character-pair substitution ratios
topic.how: Beta-Binomial mixture model fitted to raw character counts without access to labels, unsupervised clustering, cross-validated prediction
topic.why_matters: Validates a widely-cited but rarely-tested cryptographic hypothesis about manuscript structure using explicit null hypotheses and modern statistical methods

characteristics.what_it_measures: Unsupervised clustering agreement with Currier labels
characteristics.evidence: Beta-Binomial mixture selects k=2 by BIC, recovers Currier partition
characteristics.context: Fitted without labels; 113/185 folios >90% posterior confidence

characteristics.what_it_measures: Supervised classification accuracy on held-out folios
characteristics.context: Best estimate of true predictive power balancing class imbalance
Supervised Beta-Binomial classifier on training folds

characteristics.what_itasures: Effect size of character pair association with A/B
characteristics.evidence: Six of eleven pairs exceed all 1000 random permutations
characteristics.context: Mean V=0.136 exceeds every shuffled value (simulated mean 0.025)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Variance in character-pair ratios explained by A/B label
characteristics.evidence: A/B label predicts expected ratios on unseen folios
characteristics.context: 29% explained; 71% residual structure beyond binary split

characteristics.what_it_measures: Statistical significance of within-quire language transitions
characteristics.evidence: Language changes within quire produce 58% larger ratio jumps
characteristics.context: Rules out codicological confound (quire boundaries) as explanation

characteristics.what_itasures: Prediction accuracy from early to late folios
characteristics.context: Asymmetry due to class imbalance; late manuscript predominantly B

characteristics.evidence: Homogeneous source produces 3-11x less inter-folio variance

requires: minimum 20 tokens per pair per folio

novelty: First quantitative confirmation of Currier's 1976 A/B hypothesis with explicit null models
Beta-Binomial generative mixture for character-pair ratio analysis
Unsupervised recovery of A/B split without access to labels (ARI=0.383)
Three-regime taxonomy: categorical, intermediate, free-variation character pairs
Demonstration that distinction is intrinsic property, not codicological artifact
